fault_hypothesis: The comparison on line 70 uses `>` instead of `>=`, so when the number of recent events exactly equals the limit, the function incorrectly allows the request instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When recent event count equals limit, both > and >= should agree; this is FALSE only when count == limit (the failing case).
After the else branch (not denied), recent count must be strictly less than limit; fails when count equals limit.
If recent equals limit, the function should deny — so reaching the return True path with count==limit is inconsistent; this flags the boundary condition.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(recent__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when recent events meet or exceed the limit.
